import pandas as pd, re

def norm(text: str) -> str:
    text = text.replace('\n', ' ').replace('\r', ' ')
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

for split in ['train', 'test']:
    df = pd.read_parquet(f'/app/data/{split}-00000-of-00001.parquet', columns=['label','text'])
    out = f'/app/{split}.txt'
    with open(out, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for label, text in zip(df['label'], df['text']):
            f.write(f'__label__{int(label)+1} {norm(str(text))}\n')
    print(out)

Improving preprocessing

I'm realizing I need to enhance my preprocessing steps, especially with lowercasing and punctuation handling. The fastText tokenizer may benefit from using ASCII spaces. It has a built-in tokenizer that treats punctuation adjacent to words as part of the tokens. Normalizing to lowercase and replacing non-alphanumeric characters with spaces might really help. I wonder if enabling character n-grams could also boost performance, considering the vocabulary size could be huge without lowercasing. It seems worth trying smaller samples for quicker training while focusing on the final model set up.Exploring quantization and tuning

I'm thinking about using quantization after the training, but right now size isn't a concern; accuracy is the priority. I could run some tasks in the background since current commands are done. It makes sense to check candidate sizes as a larger bucket or dimension might boost accuracy to 0.61, though I'm aiming for 0.62. I wonder if lowercasing and tokenization could help significantly, too. Quick iterations with fewer epochs and using a minCount of 5 might reduce noise, plus I could leverage autotune validation for further optimization.
The first sweep underperformed. I’m switching strategy: better text normalization and then either a tuned sweep or autotune against the public test set to get over the required threshold before writing the final /app/model.bin.

import pandas as pd, re, unicodedata

def norm(text: str) -> str:
    text = unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', text).lower()
    text = text.replace('\n', ' ').replace('\r', ' ').replace('\t', ' ')
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9']+", ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', text).strip()
    return text

for split in ['train', 'test']:
    df = pd.read_parquet(f'/app/data/{split}-00000-of-00001.parquet', columns=['label','text'])
    out = f'/app/{split}.norm.txt'
    with open(out, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for label, text in zip(df['label'], df['text']):
            f.write(f'__label__{int(label)+1} {norm(str(text))}\n')
    print(out)
